mission.title: open-webui: issue: "Edit" and "Continue Response" don't work properly
mission.description: issue: "Edit" and "Continue Response" don't work properly. This issue affects 37+ users.

steps.title: Identify open-webui troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your open-webui deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui -n open-webui --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review open-webui configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui
```
### Check Existing Issues

### Installation Method

Docker

### Open WebUI Version

0.8.3

### Ollama Version (if applicable)

0.16.1

### Operating System

Windows 11

### Browser (if applicable)

Chrome 145

### Confirmation

- Start with the

steps.title: Apply the fix for issue: "Edit" and "Continue Response" don't work properly
steps.description:
Test for the obvious which is what this issue is about

Continue response
Edit message

But also check around message branching 
Edit your own message to create message branches and check if the correct branch is sent with the correct edited assistant message or correct continued response. And same if you branch the assistant message by regenerating the assistant message.

So yeah everything around message branching here should also be tested if that still behaves the same way and the fix

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm issue: "Edit" and "Continue Response" don't work… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui -n open-webui --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Test for the obvious which is what this issue is about

Continue response
Edit message

But also check around message branching 
Edit your own message to create message branches and check if the correct branch is sent with the correct edited assistant message or correct continued response. And same if you branch the assistant message by regenerating the assistant message.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-webui installed or the issue environment reproducible.
